name: Sunday Long Flow — Yin/Restorative (25-30 min)
description: **Permanent addition to the functional-bulk weekly schedule (W12D1 discussion outcome).** Long-hold yin/restorative session for the connective tissue. Morning flows are activation-grade (30-60s holds). This is *release-grade* — 90s to 2min holds, where fascia and joint capsule actually start to lengthen. Goes on the Sunday rest day. Three blocks: opening / hips / spine + shoulders / closing. Goal is parasympathetic shift + connective tissue catch-up after a heavy gym week. No equipment, floor only, can do at home.

dayOverview.theme: Yin-style long-hold flow for connective-tissue recovery. Morning flows wake the body; this lengthens it. 90s-2min holds are what fascia responds to — anything shorter is mobility prep, not release.
watchpoints: **Long holds, not stretches.** Don't push to end-range and bounce. Find ~70% of available range and STAY. The release happens in time, not in depth.
**Breathe long and slow.** 4-6 breaths per minute. The parasympathetic shift IS the intervention — recovery happens in parasympathetic state, not from the stretch itself.
**Skip anything that lights up.** If pigeon or 90/90 triggers the right-shoulder grip pattern or QL spasm, bail and switch to a milder shape.
**Pair with the post-gym cool-down**, don't substitute. End-of-session cool-down is activation-release after load; Sunday flow is dedicated recovery on a non-training day. Different jobs.

sets.name: Opening — Wake the System (~5 min)

exercises.notes: Slow, full ROM. Wake the spine segment-by-segment. Match the wave to slow breathing — inhale on cow, exhale on cat.
Knees wide, big toes touching. Arms forward, forehead on the floor. Breathe into the back ribs — that's where parasympathetic shift starts.

Knees under hips, chest sinks toward the floor with arms extended forward. Long hold of the lat + shoulder opener. **Especially relevant** for the W12 shoulder pattern — opens the pec minor/lat fascial line.

sets.notes: Get the system parasympathetic before going into the long holds. Don't rush.

sets.name: Hips — Long Holds for the QL/Glute/Hip-Rotator Pattern (~10 min)

exercises.notes: Soles together, knees out. Hinge gently from the hips — don't round the spine. Inner thigh + groin lengthen.
**90s each side.** Front leg + back leg at 90°. Sink hips toward the floor. Long hold targets the hip-rotator capsule — what gets gripped during squats and KB work. Switch sides at 90s.

**120s each side** (vs. morning flow's 30-60s). The long hold is what releases the glute + QL chain. **W12 QL watchpoint:** if the QL spasm pattern fires, drop to figure-4 (supine, ankle on knee) instead. Switch sides at 120s.
On the back, knees toward armpits, hands grip outside of feet (or shins). Gentle rock side to side or stay still. Releases sacrum + lower back.

sets.notes: Hip block is the longest because the lower body takes the heaviest loaded work. QL, glutes, hip flexors all get pinned in flexion patterns during squats and deadlifts.

sets.name: Spine + Shoulders — Release the Upper-Trap/Pec/Neck Cluster (~7 min)

exercises.notes: **90s each side.** Quadruped, thread one arm under the other, rest the shoulder on the floor. Thoracic rotation + posterior shoulder lengthen. Directly addresses the W12 neck-shoulder-pec cluster pattern.
**90s each side.** Lie back, draw one knee across the body, opposite arm extended. Long hold lets the spinal erectors and obliques actually lengthen. Breathe into the rib cage on the rotated side.
Legs extended forward, hinge from the hips. Don't pull to depth — find a 70% range and stay. Posterior chain + low back decompression.

**60s each at LOW / MID / HIGH elbow positions.** All three positions to hit different fibers of pec minor + major. This is the direct intervention for the W12 right-shoulder pattern — pec minor lengthening decompresses the brachial plexus.

sets.notes: Upper body block targets exactly the cluster from W12D1: neck + shoulder + right pec as one unit. Long holds, three angles on the pec via doorframe.

sets.name: Closing — Parasympathetic Reset (~3 min)

exercises.notes: Final return to child's pose. Long slow breaths, forehead on the floor or a block.

sets.notes: **Optional 2-5 min savasana after** — lie flat on the back, palms up, eyes closed. Not 'rest,' deliberate stillness. This is when the parasympathetic shift finishes consolidating. Worth the time.

tips: **Long holds (90s-2min), not stretches.** 70% of available range, stay. Release happens in time.
**Breathe slow.** 4-6 breaths per minute. The parasympathetic shift IS the intervention.
**Hip block is the longest** — pigeon 2 min/side is non-negotiable for the QL/glute pattern.
**Pec doorframe at 3 positions** addresses the W12 right-shoulder cluster directly.
**Pair with weekday morning flows + post-gym cool-down**, don't substitute. Three layers of recovery, different jobs.
**Skip anything that lights up symptoms** — pigeon/QL or doorframe/shoulder. Switch to a milder shape rather than push.
**Optional savasana 2-5 min after** is when the parasympathetic shift consolidates. Worth the time.
formPhilosophy: Yin-style long-hold work for connective tissue recovery. Morning flows activate. Post-gym cool-down decompresses. Sunday long flow lengthens. Three layers, different timescales. Added W12 after the user surfaced the new-muscle + universal-tightness pattern + the right-shoulder cluster. The connective tissue lags muscle adaptation by 6-8 weeks; this is the input that lets it catch up.
